core_concept: Body Systems Introduction: Heart, Lungs, and Stomach as Parts of Larger Systems
facts: The heart is a pump that pushes blood to every part of the body, including the fingers and toes; if the heart stops, blood cannot reach those parts.
The lungs take in oxygen from the air and pass it into the blood; the heart then pumps that oxygen-rich blood to all body parts.
The stomach breaks down food so the body can get energy; but without oxygen from the lungs and blood from the heart, the body cannot use that energy.
misconceptions: The stomach, heart, and lungs each do their jobs completely on their own; food digestion does not need oxygen from the lungs or blood from the heart.
